## 🌎 Geopolitics

### Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire While Maintaining Naval Blockade; Iran Seizes Ships in Strait of Hormuz

President Trump announced on April 22 that the United States is extending the ceasefire with Iran—brokered April 7-8 after 54 days of conflict that began February 28—until Tehran submits a proposal to end the war. The U.S. maintains its naval blockade on Iranian ports despite Iran's demand that lifting it is a precondition for negotiations. In response, Iran seized two container ships in the Strait of Hormuz on April 22, signaling willingness to escalate rather than capitulate over blockade terms.

**Why it matters.** The Strait of Hormuz carries roughly 20% of global oil supply, and the blockade-plus-counter-seizure dynamic is already creating fuel shortages across Asia and pushing Brent crude higher. For your quant lens, the structural question is whether this becomes a permanent geopolitical risk premium or collapses within weeks—and India's vulnerability is acute: India imports 70% of its crude needs and is already exposed to supply shocks. The broader play: energy inflation ripples through Indian manufacturing costs, import bills, and currency pressure when crude spikes. Your consulting clients in energy and logistics will feel this immediately.

**Action.** Monitor EIA's weekly petroleum reports for Strait of Hormuz throughput; track Brent-WTI spreads for blockade premium. Check whether ONGC, Indian Oil, or Reliance have issued earnings guidance adjustments for crude price assumptions. Read Al Jazeera's four-scenario breakdown to model negotiation timelines against oil volatility.

*Sources: [[https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/4/22/iran-war-whats-happening-on-day-54-as-trump-extends-ceasefire](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/4/22/iran-war-whats-happening-on-day-54-as-trump-extends-ceasefire)]([https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/4/22/iran-war-whats-happening-on-day-54-as-trump-extends-ceasefire](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/4/22/iran-war-whats-happening-on-day-54-as-trump-extends-ceasefire)) · [[https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2026/04/22/hormuz-strait-us-iran-talks-war/](https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2026/04/22/hormuz-strait-us-iran-talks-war/)]([https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2026/04/22/hormuz-strait-us-iran-talks-war/](https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2026/04/22/hormuz-strait-us-iran-talks-war/)) · [[https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/4/21/iran-us-war-four-scenarios-for-whats-next-as-talks-stumble](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/4/21/iran-us-war-four-scenarios-for-whats-next-as-talks-stumble)]([https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/4/21/iran-us-war-four-scenarios-for-whats-next-as-talks-stumble](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/4/21/iran-us-war-four-scenarios-for-whats-next-as-talks-stumble))*

### South Korea President Visits India to Deepen Tech and Defense Ties; Targets $50 Billion Bilateral Trade by 2030

South Korean President Lee Jae Myung met Prime Minister Narendra Modi at Hyderabad House on April 21—the first Seoul presidential visit to India in eight years—with explicit goals to nearly double bilateral trade to $50 billion by 2030. The partnership prioritizes semiconductors, shipbuilding, artificial intelligence, defense manufacturing, and supply chain resilience. Lee described the partnership as critical in "an era of hyper uncertainty," positioning the two nations as "ideal partners for comprehensive cooperation."

**Why it matters.** This directly touches your ecosystem: India is building a semiconductor alternative to Taiwan-China dependence, and South Korea brings Samsung-class chip expertise plus defense and AI capabilities India desperately needs. The $50B target implies real capital—roughly 2x current levels—which signals serious US-India supply chain reshoring momentum. For your quant and consulting lens, this validates India's structural arbitrage: lower labor costs than Taiwan, geopolitical alignment with the US, and position between energy suppliers (Gulf) and consumer markets (Southeast Asia). Semiconductors and AI are core to India's 2030 tech ambitions, and Seoul's partnership accelerates the timeline. This affects everything from FDI flows into Indian tech parks to talent competition in Hyderabad.

**Action.** Track NASSCOM announcements for Indo-SK investment MoUs and project timelines. Monitor Samsung and SK Hynix India expansion roadmaps—watch for wafer fab capacity commitments or design center scaling in Bangalore or Hyderabad. Review Indian defense stocks (BEL, HAL) and semiconductor plays (TCS, Infosys semiconductor divisions) for upside from this partnership. Read the CNBC article for the structural barriers to $50B—tariff asymmetries, skill gaps, and forex volatility will determine execution.

*Sources: [[https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/21/india-south-korea-aim-to-deepen-ties-amid-geopolitical-uncertainty-here-is-whats-holding-them-back.html](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/21/india-south-korea-aim-to-deepen-ties-amid-geopolitical-uncertainty-here-is-whats-holding-them-back.html)]([https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/21/india-south-korea-aim-to-deepen-ties-amid-geopolitical-uncertainty-here-is-whats-holding-them-back.html](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/21/india-south-korea-aim-to-deepen-ties-amid-geopolitical-uncertainty-here-is-whats-holding-them-back.html)) · [[https://www.csis.org/analysis/indias-future-strategic-choices-complications-mass](https://www.csis.org/analysis/indias-future-strategic-choices-complications-mass)]([https://www.csis.org/analysis/indias-future-strategic-choices-complications-mass](https://www.csis.org/analysis/indias-future-strategic-choices-complications-mass))*

## 🧠 Artificial Intelligence

### Google splits TPU 8 into dedicated training and inference chips at Cloud Next 2026

Google announced two 8th-generation TPU variants at Cloud Next on April 22: TPU 8t (training) and TPU 8i (inference). A single TPU 8t superpod scales to 9,600 chips with 2 petabytes of shared high-bandwidth memory, delivering 121 ExaFLOPS; each chip carries 216 GB of HBM at 6.5 TB/s and 12.6 petaFLOPS of FP4 compute. The TPU 8i is the inference chip, carrying 384 MB of on-chip SRAM — triple Ironwood's allocation — plus 288 GB HBM at 8.6 TB/s, targeting 80% price-performance improvement over Ironwood at low-latency workloads. Both deliver 2x better performance-per-watt and are targeting general availability later in 2026.

**Why it matters.** The two-chip split is an architectural admission that training and inference have diverged enough to warrant completely different silicon profiles. The TPU 8i's 384 MB of on-chip SRAM isn't a random spec bump — large SRAM directly attacks the MoE (mixture-of-experts) inference bottleneck, where expert routing requires low-latency random access that HBM bandwidth alone can't satisfy. If you are today deciding whether to build inference infrastructure on NVIDIA H200/Rubin or on Google Cloud TPUs, the TPU 8i's SRAM and per-token cost numbers matter for exactly the large sparse models (Gemini 3.1, GPT-5.4) dominating production workloads. Google also announced a $750M partner fund and confirmed a renewed NVIDIA partnership, meaning hybrid Rubin+TPU 8 deployments are now explicitly supported — the binary TPU-vs-NVIDIA framing is dissolving.

**Action.** Read the Google Cloud TPU 8t/8i technical deep dive blog before making any 2026 H2 infrastructure commitments. If you run MoE inference today, benchmark your expert-routing latency against Ironwood baseline — that's the specific bottleneck the TPU 8i SRAM is designed to eliminate, and the perf-per-dollar claim holds most strongly there. The 2.8x training price-performance claim on TPU 8t warrants a re-evaluation of your pre-training or fine-tuning cost models if you've been locked into A100/H100 pricing assumptions.

*Sources: [cloud.google.com](https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/compute/tpu-8t-and-tpu-8i-technical-deep-dive) · [techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/22/google-cloud-next-new-tpu-ai-chips-compete-with-nvidia/) · [cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/22/google-launches-training-and-inference-tpus-in-latest-shot-at-nvidia.html)*

---

### OpenAI ships Codex-powered workspace agents that run persistently in the cloud without a user present

On April 22, OpenAI introduced workspace agents in ChatGPT, available in research preview on Business, Enterprise, Edu, and Teachers plans. These are Codex-powered agents (Codex being OpenAI's code-execution and tool-use backbone) that teams build once, share across an org via ChatGPT or Slack, and which run continuously in the cloud between user sessions — drafting code, routing feedback, pulling metrics on a schedule. Pricing is free through May 6, 2026, then switches to credit-based billing.

**Why it matters.** The architectural distinction from prior GPTs is persistence: these agents are not request-response, they are long-horizon task executors that continue running when no one is watching and request human approval at decision gates. For an engineer currently building custom agentic pipelines — task planners, tool-calling loops, approval checkpoints — this is the clearest signal yet that OpenAI is productizing the orchestration layer rather than just the model layer. The Codex integration means agents inherit code execution natively; the Slack connector means the action surface extends into enterprise communication channels without custom integration. If your consulting practice sells "custom AI agent" implementations at the workflow-automation layer, the build-vs-buy calculus just shifted.

**Action.** Test the workspace agents research preview against a real internal workflow before May 6 while it is free — specifically target a multi-step process that currently requires a human to return to the tool to advance each stage, since persistence is the new capability the API alone does not trivially give you. If you already have agentic workflows built on the Assistants API or Codex API, read OpenAI's workspace agents architecture notes carefully for overlap: the risk is paying twice for orchestration you already built.

*Sources: [openai.com](https://openai.com/index/introducing-workspace-agents-in-chatgpt/) · [9to5mac.com](https://9to5mac.com/2026/04/22/openai-updates-chatgpt-with-codex-powered-workspace-agents-for-teams/) · [help.openai.com](https://help.openai.com/en/articles/11391654-chatgpt-business-release-notes)*

## 🇮🇳 India

### AWS Opens SageMaker Access to India's AI Startups Through IndiaAI Mission Partnership

Amazon Web Services announced on April 20 an infrastructure partnership with SHI India under the government's IndiaAI Mission, making AWS SageMaker and AI services directly available to startups, enterprises, and research organizations building indigenous large language models. The collaboration removes the infrastructure barrier for developers wanting to train and customize LLMs without managing complex backend systems.

**Why it matters.** An AI engineer in Hyderabad building an LLM startup now has immediate access to production-grade SageMaker capabilities through the government's IndiaAI framework without needing to negotiate enterprise contracts or manage infrastructure complexity. This shifts the bottleneck from "can we afford GPU infrastructure" to "can we build better models." Consulting engineers can offer SageMaker-based AI implementations to enterprises faster. The official sanctioning through IndiaAI also signals regulatory tailwinds for indigenous model development rather than relying entirely on OpenAI or Anthropic APIs.

**Action.** Register your startup with the IndiaAI Mission portal to confirm eligibility for AWS partnership benefits. Pull the AWS SageMaker documentation to understand available pre-trained models and fine-tuning workflows. If building a deeptech AI company, log the ₹3 crore revenue threshold for maintaining startup status (doubled from ₹1 crore in recent policy changes) to plan runway accordingly.

*Sources: [[AWS Press Center](https://press.aboutamazon.com/aws/2026/4/aws-collaborates-with-shi-india-to-enable-ai-infrastructure-for-indiaai-mission)]([AWS Press Center](https://press.aboutamazon.com/aws/2026/4/aws-collaborates-with-shi-india-to-enable-ai-infrastructure-for-indiaai-mission)) · [[CRN Asia](https://www.crnasia.com/india/news/2026/aws-partners-with-empanelled-shi-india-to-support-ai-infrastructure-under-indiaai-mission)]([CRN Asia](https://www.crnasia.com/india/news/2026/aws-partners-with-empanelled-shi-india-to-support-ai-infrastructure-under-indiaai-mission))*

### Tech Mahindra Q4 Closes FY26 With ₹1,353.8 Crore Profit, $3.7B in Largest Deal Wins in Five Years

Tech Mahindra reported Q4 FY26 net profit of ₹1,353.8 crore, up 16 percent year-on-year, with quarterly revenue of ₹15,076 crore (12.6% growth) and record-breaking full-year deal wins of $3,794 million—the highest in the past five years. EBIT margins expanded to 13.8 percent, gaining 70 basis points quarter-on-quarter on the back of mega deal closures.

**Why it matters.** Tier-1 IT services companies reporting record deal wins signals sustained enterprise demand for transformation services, creating immediate hiring and consulting opportunities for AI engineers across implementation, architecture, and emerging capability teams. The ₹15,000 crore quarterly revenue base shows where the money is flowing—if you're exploring contract roles or joining a consulting firm to build AI applications at enterprise scale, this sector stability matters. Tech Mahindra's momentum also reflects rupee depreciation tailwinds that make Indian delivery competitive globally, tightening the labor market for skilled AI engineers.

**Action.** Monitor Tech Mahindra and peer (TCS, Infosys, HCL Tech) earnings for AI hiring announcements and capability investments—Infosys reports April 23, TCS reported strong deal wins at $40.7 billion for FY26. Check Tech Mahindra careers for GenAI specialist roles; mega deals typically unlock new hiring within 60-90 days. Track whether mega-deal revenues are flowing to AI-adjacent practices versus legacy IT services.

*Sources: [[Business Standard](https://www.business-standard.com/companies/quarterly-results/tech-mahindra-q4-net-profit-up-16-per-cent-yoy-fy26-results-126042200816_1.html)]([Business Standard](https://www.business-standard.com/companies/quarterly-results/tech-mahindra-q4-net-profit-up-16-per-cent-yoy-fy26-results-126042200816_1.html)) · [[APAC News Network](https://apacnewsnetwork.com/2026/04/tech-mahindra-q4-results-fy26-net-profit-jumps-16-to-rs-1353-8-crore-declares-rs-15-per-share-dividend)]([APAC News Network](https://apacnewsnetwork.com/2026/04/tech-mahindra-q4-results-fy26-net-profit-jumps-16-to-rs-1353-8-crore-declares-rs-15-per-share-dividend))*

## 📈 Markets

### US Records on Iran Ceasefire Push Stocks Higher; India Stumbles on IT Stress and Oil Shock

Wall Street closed at fresh records Wednesday on Trump's Iran ceasefire extension: S&P 500 7,137.90 (+1.05%), Nasdaq 24,657.57 (+1.64%), Dow 49,490.03 (+0.69%), buoyed by a rush of earnings and de-escalation relief. India's Nifty 50 fell to 24,378.10, snapping a three-day win streak, with the Sensex sinking 757 points to 78,516 as IT heavyweights sold off. Brent crude rebounded sharply to $101.91 from a $98.48 close amid renewed shipping attacks near Iran, while gold retreated below $4,750 per ounce—down roughly 10% since the conflict escalated.

**Why it matters.** The bifurcation signals a widening liquidity divergence. US risk appetite surges on geopolitical relief, but India faces a macro squeeze: RBI kept the repo rate anchored at 5.25% on April 8 while oil prices reignite inflation pressures, narrowing room for rate cuts despite a 6.9% FY27 GDP growth forecast (down from 7.6% in FY26). RBI's 4.6% CPI projection for FY27 looks optimistic if Brent holds the $101 level; March CPI already ticked to 3.4%. This matters for prop traders: the INR faces headwinds from commodity rebound and sticky US yields even as Fed patience extends, crowding out traditional inflation hedges like gold.

**Action.** Watch for India's April-May inflation print (due late May) against RBI's next MPC pause in June. Monitor Brent's hold above $100—a sustained breach signals CPI pressure that could force RBI off the sidelines into tightening, compressing carry trades. Check US 10-year yield reaction to next week's PCE data and Fed speakers; a hawkish surprise would tighten the yield corridor, pinning INR weakness deeper.

*Sources: [[Business Standard](https://www.business-standard.com/markets/news/stock-market-live-april-22-nse-bse-sensex-today-nifty-gift-nifty-us-iran-talks-nestle-hcltech-q4-results-126042200090_1.html)]([Business Standard](https://www.business-standard.com/markets/news/stock-market-live-april-22-nse-bse-sensex-today-nifty-gift-nifty-us-iran-talks-nestle-hcltech-q4-results-126042200090_1.html)) · [[Yahoo Finance US Markets](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/live/stock-market-today-wednesday-april-22-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-trump-us-iran-ceasefire-230429476.html)]([Yahoo Finance US Markets](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/live/stock-market-today-wednesday-april-22-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-trump-us-iran-ceasefire-230429476.html)) · [[Investing.com Brent](https://www.investing.com/commodities/brent-oil)]([Investing.com Brent](https://www.investing.com/commodities/brent-oil)) · [[RBI Monetary Policy April 2026](https://taxguru.in/rbi/rbi-monetary-policy-april-2026-key-rates-measures.html)]([RBI Monetary Policy April 2026](https://taxguru.in/rbi/rbi-monetary-policy-april-2026-key-rates-measures.html))*

---

### Brent at $101 Threatens RBI's Inflation Comfort; Gold Collapse Reflects Sticky Yields

Brent crude's jump to $101.91 from $98.48 reflects supply anxiety over Middle East shipping attacks, compressing the margin of safety in RBI's inflation projections. Gold's 10% decline since the conflict began signals capital is repricing away from inflation hedges into rate-sensitive assets; the metal traded below $4,750 per ounce Wednesday as US yields remained sticky despite Fed dovish signaling.

**Why it matters.** The commodity-gold divergence is a tell on where real yields are headed. Oil strength tightens the CPI ceiling for RBI just as monetary easing hopes were building into next year; a sustained $100+ Brent pencils in an extra 30-50 basis points of inflation risk for FY27, curtailing the central bank's $1.2 trillion in excess liquidity absorption. Gold's capitulation suggests investors are pricing zero real rates—or even shallow negative real rates—off sticky nominal yields, a confirmation that Fed tightness will persist. For INR carry trades, this is toxic: commodity costs rising while hedging instruments (gold, long-dated bonds) price out.

**Action.** Track oil inventory data (EIA Wednesday releases) for demand signals that could push Brent above $105. Watch for RBI language on commodity pass-through at the next MPC briefing (June); any shift from "transitory" to "persistent" signals a policy pivot. INR traders should eye the 93.50 USD level on the rupee—a break above there compounds the cost of carry as both commodity costs and rate differentials work against the currency.

*Sources: [[Trading Economics Brent Crude](https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil)]([Trading Economics Brent Crude](https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil)) · [[Gold Price Data](https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/gold)]([Gold Price Data](https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/gold)) · [[RBI Inflation Projections](https://www.ceicdata.com/en/india/rbi-monetary-policy-statement-inflation-projection/cpi-inflation-projection-annual)]([RBI Inflation Projections](https://www.ceicdata.com/en/india/rbi-monetary-policy-statement-inflation-projection/cpi-inflation-projection-annual))*

## 💹 Stocks

### INFY (NSE/NYSE): -3.4% intraday / ADR -5.2% post-close as FY27 guidance of 2-5% CC falls well short of street's 7-9%

Infosys (NSE: INFY) closed April 23 at ₹1,268.60, down 3.4% from its prior close of ₹1,313.20, as the NIFTY IT complex stayed under pressure into the post-market earnings event. After the bell, the company issued FY27 revenue guidance of 2-5% in constant currency — the most conservative opening bid the company has delivered in years, against a street consensus running at 7-9% CC — sending the NYSE ADR (INFY) down a further 5.2% in US trade. Q4 FY26 itself was broadly in line: sell-side consensus had penciled in revenue of roughly ₹46,567 crore (+13.7% year-on-year) and PAT near ₹7,509 crore (+4% YoY), with EBIT margins expected to hold in the 20.5-21% band. The quarter is not the story; the guide is.

**Why it matters.** The 2-5% CC guide lands on top of similarly cautious prints from TCS, HCLTech, and Wipro this quarter, closing the door on any sector re-rating narrative for now. Two structural forces are at work simultaneously: US tariff uncertainty is causing large enterprise clients to pause deal ramp-ups mid-cycle, and Generative AI is compressing legacy application maintenance billings — the same workflows are being serviced with fewer billable hours. For Indian mid-cap IT (Mphasis, LTIMindtree, Coforge), the read-across is worse: if Infosys with a diversified $20B+ mega-deal book caps out at 5%, peers running narrower vertical mixes face a tighter ceiling. For an AI engineer watching this sector, the paradox is structural — the GenAI toolchain you help build is directly undercutting the revenue model of the largest buyer of your company's compute.

**Action.** Infosys will hold an investor call; the transcript and full financial statements will be posted at infosys.com/investors/reports-filings/quarterly-results/2025-2026/q4.html. The formal exchange filing under SEBI LODR will be on the NSE and BSE disclosure portals. The next technical level to watch on NSE is the ₹1,200 zone, which corresponds to multi-year support last tested in December 2020. FY27 guidance upgrades (or the absence of them at the first quarter print) will be the primary re-rating catalyst to track.

*Sources: [infosys.com](https://www.infosys.com/investors/reports-filings/quarterly-results/2025-2026/q4.html) · [whalesbook.com](https://www.whalesbook.com/news/English/tech/Infosys-Lowers-FY27-Growth-Outlook-Due-to-AI-and-Economy-Fears/69e4f110bca97ee10691f439) · [business-standard.com](https://www.business-standard.com/markets/news/infosys-share-price-nse-bse-q4-results-profit-revenue-cc-gen-ai-iran-war-126042200102_1.html)*

---

### GEV (NYSE): +13.0% as Q1 data center power orders in a single quarter exceed all of 2025 combined

GE Vernova (NYSE: GEV) surged from a prior close of $991.30 to approximately $1,119 on April 22 — a gain of roughly 13.0% — after posting Q1 2026 revenue of $9.30 billion, up 16% year-on-year and ahead of the $9.27 billion consensus. Adjusted EBITDA came in at $900 million, a 10% margin representing 390 basis points of year-on-year expansion. Total orders hit $18.3 billion on 71% organic growth, and management raised full-year 2026 guidance across all metrics: revenue to $44.5B-$45.5B (from $44B-$45B), EBITDA margin to 12-14% (from 11-13%), and free cash flow to $6.5B-$7.5B (from $5B-$5.5B). Reported GAAP EPS of $17.44 included a $4.5 billion pre-tax remeasurement gain from the Prolec GE acquisition; adjusted EPS was $1.98 against a $1.90 consensus estimate.

**Why it matters.** The most consequential line in the release for the AI infrastructure trade is this: GEV's electrification segment booked $2.4 billion in data center equipment orders during Q1 2026 alone — exceeding the full-year 2025 total — with a 2.5x book-to-bill ratio on that segment. This is a real-time confirmation, from a physical-layer supplier, that hyperscaler power capex is accelerating rather than pausing despite macro headwinds. The India read-across is direct: ABB India, Siemens India, and Hitachi Energy India are exposed to the same global data center buildout through transformer and switchgear demand, and NIFTY infrastructure and capital goods names should track GEV's order momentum as a leading indicator. For an AI engineer watching the semis complex, GEV validates what chip lead times and NVLink cluster deployments already imply: the physical power and grid interconnect layer is now the binding constraint on AI capacity expansion, not silicon.

**Action.** GEV will file its 10-Q with the SEC at sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar. The earnings transcript, posted on ge.com/investors, is worth reading for management commentary on transformer delivery timelines and whether grid interconnect bottlenecks are extending into 2027. The contrast trade is worth noting: GE Aerospace (NYSE: GE) fell 5% on the same day because management held full-year guidance unchanged, citing airline demand uncertainty — a reminder that the power and data center slice of the old GE empire is now repricing at a material premium to the aerospace slice.

*Sources: [247wallst.com](https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/04/22/ge-vernova-zooms-13-ge-aerospace-loses-5-as-earnings-day-fractures-the-family/) · [quiverquant.com](https://www.quiverquant.com/news/GE+VERNOVA+($GEV)+Releases+Q1+2026+Earnings) · [tradingview.com](https://www.tradingview.com/news/tradingview:f5c0de3f3d520:0-ge-vernova-10-q-revenues-9-34b-eps-17-44/)*

## 🏗 Tech & Startups

### Razorpay Plans Confidential IPO Filing, Targeting $600-700 Million at $5-6 Billion Valuation

Bengaluru-based fintech giant Razorpay is preparing to file confidentially for an IPO in the coming weeks, marking a major step toward its public market debut. The payments platform is targeting a raise of $600 to $700 million with a valuation of $5 to $6 billion—a notable reset from its $7.5 billion peak in 2021. For FY25, Razorpay reported revenue of approximately $407 million (INR 37.8 billion) but posted a net loss of $130 million. The confidential filing route allows flexibility in volatile market conditions.

**Why it matters.** Razorpay's path to IPO signals that India's fintech consolidation is entering a new phase where profitability matters more than growth-at-all-costs valuation. The company's move to lower its valuation and opt for a conservative raise shows founders and investors that disciplined unit economics and sustainable revenue models are prerequisites for public markets in 2026. For builders in payments, lending, and embedded finance, this playbook—strip excess burn, focus on revenue quality, and take a measured approach to fundraising—is now the template.

**Action.** Track Razorpay's confidential filing updates via business press (Business Today, Outlook Business, Economic Times have been covering closely). Monitor the timing of the DRHP filing with SEBI, as it will reveal exact revenue multiples and profitability trajectory the market will value. Compare Razorpay's unit economics against earlier IPOs like PhonePe or Paytm to understand how payment platforms are being priced in this cycle.

*Sources: [[The Paypers](https://thepaypers.com/payments/news/razorpay-plans-confidential-ipo-filing-in-india-targeting-usd-600-mln-raise)]([The Paypers](https://thepaypers.com/payments/news/razorpay-plans-confidential-ipo-filing-in-india-targeting-usd-600-mln-raise)) · [[Outlook Business](https://www.outlookbusiness.com/corporate/razorpay-ipo-valuation-reset-600-700mn)]([Outlook Business](https://www.outlookbusiness.com/corporate/razorpay-ipo-valuation-reset-600-700mn)) · [[Business Today](https://www.businesstoday.in/markets/ipo-corner/story/razorpay-may-opt-for-confidential-ipo-filing-valuation-seen-around-5-billion-report-526411-2026-04-20)]([Business Today](https://www.businesstoday.in/markets/ipo-corner/story/razorpay-may-opt-for-confidential-ipo-filing-valuation-seen-around-5-billion-report-526411-2026-04-20))*

---

### Polaris Smart Metering Raises $80 Million From British International Investment, Scales Grid Modernization Across India

Rajasthan-based Polaris Smart Metering closed an $80 million funding round led by UK-based British International Investment, a development finance institution. The deeptech company operates in the smart metering and grid modernization space, providing critical infrastructure for electricity distribution networks across India. The investment signals rising appetite from DFIs and impact investors for infrastructure-as-code plays in emerging markets where government digitalization mandates are creating massive TAMs.

**Why it matters.** Grid modernization is the unsexy deeptech that unlocks every other energy transition story—solar deployment, EV charging networks, demand response, and microgrids all need smart metering as a foundation. Polaris's funding validates that infrastructure software for utilities is fundable and scalable beyond Silicon Valley's cloud-native obsession. For founders targeting B2G2C models in energy, utilities, or critical infrastructure, this shows that patient capital from development finance institutions is available for plays with 10-year visibility and tangible social impact.

**Action.** Research Polaris Smart Metering's customer base and rollout timeline across Indian states. Track British International Investment's other infrastructure plays in India and Southeast Asia to understand the thesis. For builders in energy tech or smart infrastructure, BII and similar DFIs are increasingly active LPs—their investment criteria favor revenue visibility, regulatory tailwinds, and measurable impact metrics over hypergrowth stories.

## 🩹 Health & Science

### First Drug to Double Pancreatic Cancer Survival Breaks KRAS Therapeutic Barrier

Revolution Medicines announced Phase 3 RASolute 302 trial results showing daraxonrasib achieved median overall survival of 13.2 months versus 6.7 months with standard chemotherapy (hazard ratio 0.40) in previously treated metastatic pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma. This is the first Phase 3 trial demonstrating greater than one year overall survival benefit for pancreatic cancer, which affects 64,000 Americans annually with a 5-year survival rate below 13%.

**Why it matters.** Daraxonrasib is a catalytic KRAS inhibitor that structurally disables the cancer protein rather than blocking it—a mechanistically novel class that validates KRAS targeting across GI malignancies where mutations are common (colorectal, non-small-cell lung). The hazard ratio of 0.40 represents a 60% reduction in death risk compared to standard care. For commercial biotech, this opens funding and partnership conversations around KRAS-driven solid tumors. For patients, it's the first evidence of meaningful life extension in metastatic disease.

**Action.** Revolution Medicines plans FDA submission under Commissioner's National Priority Voucher pathway for potential accelerated review. Investors and oncology strategists should monitor AACR Annual Meeting presentations of monotherapy and combination cohorts through April. Patients with KRAS-mutant advanced pancreatic cancer should discuss daraxonrasib trial eligibility with their oncologist.

*Sources: [[Revolution Medicines press release](https://ir.revmed.com/news-releases/news-release-details/daraxonrasib-demonstrates-unprecedented-overall-survival-benefit)]([Revolution Medicines press release](https://ir.revmed.com/news-releases/news-release-details/daraxonrasib-demonstrates-unprecedented-overall-survival-benefit)) · [[STAT News – pancreatic cancer breakthrough](https://www.statnews.com/2026/04/19/daraxonrasib-pancreatic-cancer-kras-revolution-medicines-patient-story/)]([STAT News – pancreatic cancer breakthrough](https://www.statnews.com/2026/04/19/daraxonrasib-pancreatic-cancer-kras-revolution-medicines-patient-story/)) · [[Time Magazine coverage](https://time.com/article/2026/04/14/new-promise-for-treating-pancreatic-cancer/)]([Time Magazine coverage](https://time.com/article/2026/04/14/new-promise-for-treating-pancreatic-cancer/))*

### Printed Artificial Neurons Successfully Signal to Living Brain Cells

Northwestern University engineers published research in Nature Nanotechnology demonstrating that artificially printed neurons made from molybdenum disulfide semiconductors and graphene conductors can generate electrical signals that directly activate living brain cells. Testing on mouse brain tissue slices showed the flexible printed devices successfully triggered action potentials in real neurons, establishing proof of principle for synthetic-biological neural interfaces.

**Why it matters.** This is a technical inflection in brain-machine interfaces and neuroprosthetics. Prior approaches either failed to achieve clean neural communication or required rigid materials incompatible with brain tissue. The use of biocompatible aerosol-jet printing with electronic inks enables flexible, scalable fabrication of artificial neural tissue that talks natively to biology. The underlying principle—mimicking how neurons signal with minimal energy—directly informs neuromorphic computing architectures seeking to match the brain's energy efficiency, a constraint that limits current AI models.

**Action.** Neurotechnology founders should review the Nature Nanotechnology paper for design principles applicable to hearing restoration implants, visual prosthetics, and spinal cord interfaces. Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency and NIH program managers should consider this a proof point for the broader bioelectronics investment thesis. Clinical translation timeline is early stage; expect 3-5 year pathway to first human implant trials in hearing and vision.

*Sources: [[Northwestern Now](https://news.northwestern.edu/stories/2026/4/printed-neurons-communicate-with-living-brain-cells)]([Northwestern Now](https://news.northwestern.edu/stories/2026/4/printed-neurons-communicate-with-living-brain-cells)) · [[Nature Nanotechnology publication](https://www.mccormick.northwestern.edu/news/articles/2026/04/printed-neurons-communicate-with-living-brain-cells/)]([Nature Nanotechnology publication](https://www.mccormick.northwestern.edu/news/articles/2026/04/printed-neurons-communicate-with-living-brain-cells/)) · [[ScienceDaily coverage](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/04/260417225020.htm)]([ScienceDaily coverage](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/04/260417225020.htm))*

## 🎤 Culture & World

### Michael Jackson Biopic Confirms Estate-Approved Prestige as New Theatrical Play

Antoine Fuqua's Michael, starring Michael Jackson's nephew Jaafar Jackson in his acting debut, releases nationwide April 24, 2026 in IMAX after a Berlin premiere April 10. The film spans Jackson's Jackson 5 era through early solo career, with a supporting cast including Nia Long, Laura Harrier, and Colman Domingo. Critics dubbed the storytelling "sanitized," but Jaafar Jackson's performance earned universal praise.

**Why it matters.** Michael signals the entertainment industry's shift toward family-approved legacy IP as prestige theatrical properties. That a major director (Fuqua), major studio (Lionsgate/Universal), and premium format (IMAX) attached to an inherently revisionist form—the biopic—shows where the industry still believes cinema matters. The gap between critical cold shoulder and performance praise reveals the structural tension: approved narratives sidestep artistic risk, but star turns get noticed. In a streaming-first era, theaters now host sanitized heritage storytelling. This model will spawn imitators.

**Action.** Monitor Michael's theatrical hold. A strong run signals studios will greenlight more estate-blessed biopics; weak numbers suggest audiences skip approved narratives. Watch whether Jaafar Jackson lands major roles (family connections in legacy IP historically launch acting careers). Track whether other estates—Prince's, Bowie's, etc.—now shop prestige-biopic deals.

### Jazz Fest's 57-Year Run Proves Live Music's Unshakeable Cultural Grip

New Orleans Jazz & Heritage Festival opens Thursday, April 23 at Fair Grounds Race Course, running through May 3 across two eight-day weekends. Opening day features Raye, Kings of Leon, Cimafunk, Vieux Farka Toure, and Blind Boys of Alabama. Full headliners include Eagles, Stevie Nicks, Lorde, Jon Batiste, Nas, David Byrne, Tyler Childers, T-Pain, Stephen Marley, Teddy Swims, and The Isley Brothers.

**Why it matters.** Jazz Fest's lineup proves live festivals remain unmatched for cultural currency despite streaming's grip on music consumption. The 2026 bill stacks heritage acts (Eagles, Isley Brothers, Blind Boys) against Gen-Z stars (Raye, Lorde), signaling how curators now balance legacy positioning with discovery. Festivals are where music culture still congregates in physical space—tourism, retail, hospitality all track through live events. Jazz Fest specifically matters because it's New Orleans–tethered (heritage, rebuilding narrative) and genre-agnostic (jazz, rock, hip-hop coexist), making it the anti-algorithm.

**Action.** Watch Jazz Fest's 2026 attendance and ticket velocity as consumer-culture indicators. Track which artists achieve breakout discovery (Cimafunk, Raye) versus legacy reinforcement (Marley, Eagles) to understand festival curation strategy. Monitor social discourse around FOMO and local economic impact—live festival metrics now signal retail and travel behavior.
